headline: 1st Quarter Results
brief: Federal Bank reports Q1 FY27 net profit of ₹117.7cr, down 6.5% YoY despite higher interest income.
summary: The Federal Bank Ltd reported standalone net profit of ₹117.7 lakhs for Q1 FY27 (ended June 30, 2026), down from ₹86.2 lakhs in Q1 FY26. Interest earned rose to ₹723.8 lakhs from ₹668.7 lakhs YoY. Operating profit declined to ₹189.7 lakhs from ₹155.6 lakhs despite higher provisions of ₹31.8 lakhs. Capital adequacy ratio stands at 16.97% under Basel III. Gross NPA ratio improved to 1.52% from 1.91% YoY; net NPA at 0.18%. Earnings per share (basic) at ₹4.77, annualized return on assets at 0.30%.
retail_take: Modest earnings growth with stable asset quality and solid capital ratios, but lower-than-prior-year profitability and compressed margins warrant cautious assessment. Likely viewed as steady-state performance in a competitive banking environment.

headline: Dividend Declaration
brief: TotalEnergies sets 2027 dividend payment schedule with four distributions planned across the fiscal year.
summary: TotalEnergies' Board approved the dividend timetable for fiscal 2027, comprising three interim dividends (September, December, March) and one final dividend (June). Ex-dividend dates on Euronext and NYSE are staggered accordingly, with payment dates following 4-6 days later. Specific dividend amounts remain subject to Board and shareholder approval of 2026 financial statements. The announcement is routine administrative scheduling, pending final earnings approval.
retail_take: Routine administrative disclosure confirming TotalEnergies' commitment to its dividend policy. Market likely treats as neutral; actual reaction will depend on dividend quantum when formally declared against 2027 earnings performance.

headline: Glenstone Offer now only worth 70p per Share
brief: Glenstone's takeover offer for AIRE reduced to 70p per share following dividend declaration.
summary: Alternative Income REIT's board notes that Glenstone REIT's cash offer of 71.4p per share, announced 6 July 2026, is subject to adjustment for dividends paid. Following AIRE's declaration of a 1.4p interim dividend for the year ended 30 June 2026, the effective offer value is now 70.0p per share, reflecting the mechanical reduction in consideration under the Glenstone offer terms.
retail_take: Mechanical dividend adjustment to takeover offer; reduces headline value but reflects standard deal terms accounting for distributions made during offer period.

headline: Dividend Declaration
brief: TotalEnergies declares second interim dividend of €0.90/share for 2026, up 5.9% year-on-year.
summary: TotalEnergies' Board approved a second interim dividend of €0.90 per share for fiscal 2026, representing a 5.9% increase versus all dividends paid in 2025. The payment matches the first interim dividend for 2026 and aligns with the company's shareholder return policy prioritizing dividend growth linked to cash flow expansion. Ex-dividend date is December 31, 2026, with payment on January 5, 2027 (Euronext) and January 22, 2027 (NYSE).
retail_take: Progressive dividend growth demonstrates confidence in cash generation and energy demand, likely supporting the stock. However, modest 5.9% growth and cautious forward-looking language temper enthusiasm.

headline: Half-year Financial Report
brief: TotalEnergies reports 1H26 adjusted net income of $11.4bn (+47% YoY), with 2Q26 CFFO of $9.8bn (+14% QoQ) amid Middle East conflict.
summary: TotalEnergies delivered 1H26 adjusted net income of $11.4bn and CFFO of $18.4bn, both up significantly versus prior year despite 210 kboe/d production losses from Middle East conflict. 2Q26 adjusted net income of $6.0bn (+12% QoQ) was driven by higher oil prices ($91.6/b avg, +24% YoY), strong downstream refining margins, and new project ramp-ups (Brazil's Mero-4, Libya's Mabruk). CEO Patrick Pouyanné confirmed dividend priority with €0.90/share interim distribution (+5.9% YoY) and $1.5bn Q3 buyback authorization. Gearing improved to 13.1% from 15.5% in March.
retail_take: Strong cash generation and rising dividends signal robust energy demand and pricing, but Middle East production disruptions pose ongoing risk; market likely reads favourably on cash returns and deleveraging despite geopolitical headwinds.

headline: OP Pohjola's President and Group CEO Timo Ritakallio has announced that he will retire on 1 July 2027 as specified in his executive contract
Transaction in Own Shares
brief: HICL Infrastructure repurchased 750,000 shares at 134.94p on 22 July, now holding 169.3m treasury shares.
summary: HICL Infrastructure PLC purchased 750,000 ordinary shares through Investec Bank plc on 22 July 2026 at a weighted average price of 134.9404p. The company now holds 169,295,988 shares in treasury. Voting rights excluding treasury shares total 1,862,192,073. The shares are intended to be held as treasury stock, providing flexibility for future capital management or distributions.
retail_take: Share buybacks typically signal management confidence in valuation and provide capital return optionality; routine treasury management that modestly supports earnings per share accretion over time.

headline: MicroBlate™ Flex exceeds 100 clinical cases
brief: Creo Medical's MicroBlate™ Flex microwave ablation device surpasses 100 clinical cases, signalling growing adoption across UK and European centres.
summary: Creo Medical announced that its MicroBlate™ Flex flexible microwave ablation product has exceeded 100 clinical cases, reflecting increasing utilisation across leading UK and European centres. The milestone indicates growing clinician engagement and integration into clinical workflows, particularly for hard-to-reach lung tumours. The company expects case numbers to rise rapidly over the next 12 months and is building clinical evidence through ongoing studies whilst developing treatment pathways combining robotic-assisted navigation with its technology through collaboration with Intuitive Surgical.
retail_take: Early-stage adoption milestone suggests commercial traction for a differentiated minimally invasive lung cancer treatment option, though product remains in early rollout phase with profitability timeline unclear.

headline: Etcamah approved in the EU for ER+ breast cancer
brief: AstraZeneca's Etcamah approved in EU for first-line ER-positive breast cancer with CDK4/6 inhibitors.
summary: Etcamah (camizestrant), a next-generation oral SERD, gained European Commission approval for ER-positive, HER2-negative advanced breast cancer with emergent ESR1 mutations. The SERENA-6 Phase III trial showed a 56% reduction in disease progression or death risk versus standard care (HR 0.44; median PFS 16.0 vs 9.2 months). Etcamah is the 11th new medicine from AstraZeneca's expected 20 launches by 2030 and the first oral SERD compatible with all major CDK4/6 inhibitors in this setting.
retail_take: Positive catalyst: meaningful clinical efficacy in significant unmet need addressing endocrine resistance affecting ~30% of patients; strengthens AstraZeneca's oncology portfolio momentum with EU approval, US FDA review pending.

headline: Refinancing Transaction Update
brief: Victoria PLC secures 90%+ bondholder support for refinancing, enabling faster, cheaper consent solicitation process.
summary: Victoria PLC has confirmed that holders representing over 90% of its €166.6 million 3.75% senior secured notes due March 2028 have signed the Transaction Support Agreement, meeting the threshold to implement the refinancing via consent solicitation rather than a more costly alternative. Executive Chairman Geoff Wilding noted this outcome provides optimal execution speed and lower implementation costs, benefiting all stakeholders. The flooring group is advised by Lazard & Co., Latham & Watkins, and Brown Rudnick LLP.
retail_take: Strong creditor support de-risks refinancing execution and reduces financial costs; positive signal for balance sheet management, though market impact depends on refinancing terms not disclosed here.

headline: Adjustment of Subscription Rights Price
brief: Golden Prospect adjusts subscription rights price to 103.12p from 104.63p, reflecting 1.51p dividend payment.
summary: Golden Prospect Precious Metals has adjusted the Subscription Rights Price for the 1 December 2026 exercise date from 104.63 pence to 103.12 pence, reflecting a 1.51 pence per share dividend payable to shareholders. Shareholders retain the right to subscribe for one new ordinary share per five held annually. The company will continue reducing the Subscription Rights Price by dividend amounts as they are paid, with the next adjustment expected in October 2026.
retail_take: Routine administrative adjustment to reflect dividend payments; mechanically reduces subscription price by the dividend amount, maintaining shareholder economics with no material implications.

headline: Repayment of Smarter Convert Instrument
brief: Smarter Web Company repays $11.7m convertible instrument two weeks early, eliminating 7.7m potential shares.
summary: The Smarter Web Company repaid its Smarter Convert instrument to TOBAM entities approximately two weeks ahead of maturity on 23 July 2026. The company repaid $11,698,540 by selling 177.89 Bitcoin at an average price of $65,762. This eliminates 7,718,551 potential ordinary shares from dilution. The company deployed 100% of subscription proceeds into Bitcoin (exceeding the 98% minimum requirement) and now holds 2,700 Bitcoin. CEO Andrew Webley stated the company no longer believes convertible instruments represent the right capital solution.
retail_take: Early repayment removes share dilution overhang and suggests management confidence in alternative funding sources; market likely views positively as it reduces future dilution, though Bitcoin price exposure remains material.

headline: New Metro Bank facilities
brief: Mercia Asset Management secures £38m three-year facilities agreement with Metro Bank to support growth initiatives.
summary: Mercia Asset Management PLC has agreed a three-year facilities agreement with Metro Bank comprising a £13m term loan and £25m revolving facility. Interest rates are SONIA plus margins of 3.99% on the term loan and 3.50%-3.00% on the revolving facility, subject to leverage ratchet. The funds will support the Group's ongoing growth under its Mercia '27 strategic plan, with the company managing over £2.2bn in assets under management.
retail_take: Non-event. Routine refinancing facility announcement providing balance sheet flexibility for an established AIM-listed asset manager; no material surprise or concerning terms evident to the market.

headline: Change of Name to Trafalgar Minerals PLC
brief: Trafalgar Property Group rebrands to Trafalgar Minerals PLC following property disposal and board refresh to pursue reverse takeover in mining sector.
summary: Trafalgar Property Group PLC is changing its name to Trafalgar Minerals PLC, effective immediately upon regulatory approval. The rebrand follows completion of transactions on 5 May 2026, including disposal of property subsidiaries and appointment of new board leadership. The name change reflects the company's strategic pivot towards identifying and acquiring high-quality mineral assets through reverse takeover, with ticker TRAF and ISIN unchanged. CEO Martin Hull stated the company is making good progress evaluating opportunities in the mining and natural resources sector.
retail_take: Strategic repositioning from property to mining signals management confidence in sector opportunity; market reaction depends on quality of identified targets and reverse takeover terms—non-event until concrete deal emerges.

headline: Further expansion of 2026 Share Buyback Programme
brief: Somero Enterprises doubles 2026 share buyback programme to US$12m from US$6m, citing strong H1 performance and balance-sheet strength.
summary: Somero Enterprises increased its 2026 share buyback programme authorisation from US$6.0m to US$12.0m on 22 July 2026. The expansion follows better-than-expected H1 2026 trading disclosed in a 16 July trading update. The Board determined the company's balance-sheet strength and expected cash generation support additional shareholder capital returns aligned with its disciplined allocation framework. Repurchased shares will be cancelled, with purchases commencing after interim results publication on 8 September 2026.
retail_take: Positive signal: doubling the buyback reflects management confidence in trading momentum and financial position, typically supporting share price through both reduced share count and confidence messaging.

headline: Q3 Trading Update
brief: AJ Bell reports record Q3 growth with £121.5bn AUA, £3.0bn net inflows, and 762k customers.
summary: AJ Bell's Q3 saw platform AUA reach £121.5bn (up 26% YoY), with record net inflows of £3.0bn (up 43% YoY) and gross inflows of £6.0bn (up 50% YoY). Customer numbers grew 39k to 762k (up 23% YoY), driven by D2C platform adding 37k customers. Investment business AUM increased 41% YoY to £11.4bn with record net inflows of £0.8bn. The company announced a fee reduction on core MPS from 0.15% to 0.12% per annum effective 1 October 2026, citing scale benefits and competitive positioning.
retail_take: Strong organic growth across all metrics, record inflows, and proactive fee cuts signal market share gains and operational leverage. Positive read: demonstrates effective dual-channel strategy amid structural UK savings tailwinds.

headline: Statement re Intended Offers for Subscription
Swiss Post Converts to Hybrid Stamps
2026 First Interim Dividend
brief: INPP declares 2.19p first interim dividend for 2026; confirms full-year targets on track.
summary: International Public Partnerships has declared a first interim dividend of 2.19 pence per share, with ex-dividend date 13 August 2026 and payment on 15 September 2026. The Board confirmed the 2025 annual dividend target of 8.58p has been achieved and reaffirmed 2026 and 2027 targets of 8.79p and 9.01p respectively, maintaining a long-term projected annual dividend growth rate of approximately 2.5%. The FTSE 250 infrastructure investor holds over 130 projects across utility, transport, education, health, justice and digital sectors globally.
retail_take: Routine dividend announcement with no surprises; on-track delivery of stated targets and consistent 2.5% growth guidance supports income-focused investors' expectations but offers no catalysts for share appreciation.

headline: Interim Dividend Declaration
brief: Alternative Income REIT declares fourth interim dividend of 1.40p per share, meeting full-year target of 5.6p.
summary: Alternative Income REIT PLC (AIRE) has declared a fourth interim dividend of 1.40 pence per share for the quarter ended 30 June 2026, payable 14 August 2026 to shareholders registered 31 July 2026. The declaration fulfils the Board's annual dividend target of no less than 5.6 pence per share for the year ending 30 June 2026. The dividend will be distributed as a Property Income Distribution (PID). AIRE owns a diversified portfolio of UK commercial properties predominantly let on long leases with index-linked rent reviews, managed by Martley Capital.
retail_take: Achievement of full-year dividend target demonstrates dividend sustainability and income consistency, likely viewed positively by income-focused investors; signals operational resilience in UK property sector.

headline: Director/PDMR Shareholding
brief: Audioboom CEO Stuart Last exercises 15,353 share options, increasing holding to 54,926 shares (0.30%).
summary: On 22 July 2026, Stuart Last, CEO of Audioboom Group plc (AIM: BOOM), exercised options over 15,353 ordinary shares. He sold 7,000 shares at 470p (exercised at 312.5p, expiring 31 July) to fund the exercise of remaining 8,353 options at 130p. His total holding increased to 54,926 shares, representing 0.30% of issued capital, plus 323,984 unexercised options.
retail_take: CEO exercising options close to expiry and selling appreciated shares to fund further purchases suggests confidence in valuation but is largely a routine capital structure adjustment with limited forward significance.

headline: Appointment of Non-Executive Director
brief: Anglo Asian Mining appoints Cameron Pforr as Non-Executive Director, bringing cybersecurity and corporate finance expertise.
summary: Cameron Pforr, aged 61, joins Anglo Asian Mining's board effective immediately as Non-Executive Director. Pforr is currently CEO/CFO of NYSE-listed Mtron Industries and previously held senior roles at cybersecurity firms IronNet and Fidelis Cybersecurity, plus investment banking experience at Deutsche Bank, Bain & Co and Mustang Capital. Chairman Khosrow Zamani cited Pforr's public markets and international corporate finance experience as valuable to the company's growth strategy targeting 50,000-55,000 tonnes annual copper production by 2030.
retail_take: Board reinforcement with strong corporate finance and international markets credentials supports Anglo Asian's stated mid-tier producer ambitions, though cybersecurity expertise less obviously relevant to mining operations.

headline: Notification of Major Holdings
brief: Forum Energy Services increases stake in Angus Energy PLC to 25.6% from 16.2%
summary: Forum Energy Services Limited, based in Isleworth, UK, crossed the major holdings threshold on 14 July 2026, acquiring voting rights in Angus Energy PLC (ISIN GB00BYWKC989). The company now holds 2,051,394,665 ordinary shares representing 25.604% of voting rights, up from 16.211673% previously. The notification was filed with the issuer on 18 July 2026.
retail_take: Significant accumulation by Forum Energy Services suggests strategic interest or potential bid activity, though without additional context on Forum's intentions or Angus Energy's circumstances, the market impact depends on whether this is viewed as stabilizing or potentially dilutive to existing shareholders.

headline: Directorate change
brief: Apertura Energy appoints Carlos Bellorin, veteran Venezuelan oil and gas expert, as Non-Executive Director effective immediately.
summary: Apertura Energy PLC has appointed Carlos Bellorin as Non-Executive Director to strengthen its Board as the acquisition company advances its Venezuela strategy. Bellorin brings over 20 years' upstream sector experience, including legal counsel on Orinoco Oil Belt developments. Currently Executive Vice President at Welligence Energy Analytics and a former 15-year S&P Global executive, he holds an LLM in Petroleum Law and teaches International Petroleum Law at Queen Mary University London and Sciences Po Paris.
retail_take: Market likely views this positively as targeted expertise for Venezuela operations; deep sectoral knowledge and industry relationships reduce execution risk on emerging market strategy.

headline: Issue of Share Options
brief: Huddled Group issues 85.5m share options to directors and employees at 0.4p exercise price.
summary: Huddled Group PLC is implementing a share option scheme issuing 85.5m options over ordinary shares at 0.4p per share. Executive Chairman Martin Higginson receives 15m options and Group Finance Director Daniel Wortley receives 7.5m, with 63m allocated to other employees. Options vest after 12 months and when share price reaches 2.5p VWAP for 20 consecutive business days. Total options outstanding will represent 10.7% of issued capital (811.3m shares). Intended EMI scheme treatment under HMRC guidelines.
retail_take: Standard incentive scheme using EMI treatment signals confidence in future performance and share price recovery (2.5p target from 0.4p exercise price), though dilution of ~10.7% and lenient vesting conditions may concern some investors.

headline: Appointment of CEO and Trading Update
brief: Videndum appoints Jan Peter Tewes as Group CEO from 17 August 2026; H1 trading update signals caution.
summary: Jan Peter Tewes, previously CEO of Ideal Standard, joins from Villeroy & Boch with extensive brand and distribution experience at Mars and Grohe. Stephen Harris transitions to Non-Executive Chairman. H1 2026 revenues expected in line with prior year on like-for-like basis; adjusted EBITDA modestly ahead. However, full-year adjusted EBITDA guidance cut to £15-18 million (from prior expectations) due to Middle East conflict disruption, logistics costs, Feltre facility production challenges, and weak trading conditions. Net debt £39 million at 30 June 2026.
retail_take: CEO appointment is credible leadership hire, but aggressive EBITDA guidance cut and production issues dominate; suggests structural challenges requiring operational turnaround rather than growth momentum.

headline: Director/PDMR Shareholding
brief: Cranswick director Christopher Aldersley acquired 2 ordinary shares at 5415p each via employee share scheme on 21 July.
summary: Christopher Aldersley, a Director of Cranswick plc, purchased 2 ordinary shares of 10 pence each on 21 July 2026 at 5415 pence per share as Partnership Shares under the Cranswick 2024 Buy As You Earn Share Incentive Plan. The acquisition was part of a regular monthly purchase programme conducted on the London Stock Exchange.
retail_take: Routine insider share purchase via employee scheme; modest transaction size suggests modest engagement rather than significant conviction signal.

headline: Director Declaration
brief: South East Water appoints John Halsall as Chief Executive Officer effective 23 July 2026.
summary: John Halsall has taken up the role of Chief Executive Officer at South East Water following regulatory approval. He brings 25+ years of transformation and operational leadership experience, including previous roles as Chief Operating Officer at Pennon Group (overseeing South West Water, Bournemouth Water, and Bristol Water) and senior positions at Network Rail and Thames Water. Halsall has set three initial pledges: fixing day-to-day performance, driving operational excellence, and ensuring transparent engagement with stakeholders.
retail_take: Appointment of experienced water industry veteran suggests board confidence in operational turnaround; market likely views positively given sector-specific expertise and clear remediation agenda.

headline: Admission of Shares & Total Voting Rights
brief: Goldstone Resources admits 351.6m new shares to AIM following subscription from Persistence Gold Group.
summary: Goldstone Resources (AIM: GRL) has admitted 351,594,899 new ordinary shares of 1p each to trading on AIM effective 23 July 2026, following a subscription by Persistence Gold Group Ltd announced 13 July. Post-admission, total issued share capital comprises 1,677,425,013 ordinary shares with one voting right each. The new shares rank pari passu with existing shares. The company is a Ghana-focused gold producer with the Akrokeri-Homase project containing a 602,000oz JORC-compliant resource and a 50% interest in a Sierra Leone gold project.
retail_take: Significant dilution from 351.6m new shares; positive signal that subscription found backing, supporting development plans, but material share count increase will concern existing shareholders regarding earnings per share impact.

headline: Director/PDMR Shareholding
brief: Mpac awards 54,455 options to incoming CFO Duncan Tyler under LTIP scheme.
summary: Mpac Group awarded Duncan Tyler, incoming CFO, 43,564 performance-related options and 10,891 restricted stock units on 22 July 2026. Performance-related options vest based on cumulative EPS reaching 85p (nil below 70p) and ROCE reaching 15% by end-2028 (nil below 13%), weighted 50/50. Restricted stock units require three-year employment tenure and satisfactory performance. All vested shares subject to two-year holding period post-exercise.
retail_take: Routine PDMR disclosure of executive incentive grant reflecting new CFO appointment; standard long-term performance alignment mechanism with no immediate market implications.

headline: Notice of Third Quarter Trading Update
brief: Safestore to release Q3 trading update on 3 September 2026 covering three months to 31 July.
summary: Safestore Holdings plc announced it will publish its third quarter trading update on Thursday, 3 September 2026 for the period ending 31 July 2026. The self-storage operator, which operates over 200 stores across Europe, will provide trading performance metrics for the quarter. Contact details for CEO Frederic Vecchioli and CFO Simon Clinton were provided alongside FTI Consulting's investor relations team.
retail_take: Routine announcement of results publication date; provides investors with timing to expect Q3 performance data but contains no substantive trading information or forward guidance.

headline: Notice of AGM
brief: Bezant Resources announces 2026 AGM scheduled for 14 August at London venue.
summary: Bezant Resources plc, an AIM-listed exploration and development company with copper-gold projects in Namibia and Zambia plus a manganese project in Botswana, has scheduled its 2026 Annual General Meeting for Friday 14 August 2026 at 1:00 p.m. at Druces LLP, 6th Floor, 99 Gresham Street, London. The Notice of AGM with a letter from Executive Chairman Colin Bird was posted 22 July and is available on the company website.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of AGM arrangements; no material business updates or strategic announcements. Market unlikely to react as this is standard corporate governance procedure.

headline: Notice of AGM
brief: Xtract Resources plc announces Annual General Meeting scheduled for 14 August 2026 at 10:30 a.m. in London.
summary: Xtract Resources plc (AIM: XTR), a mineral exploration and development company focused on copper and critical battery metals, has scheduled its Annual General Meeting for Friday, 14 August 2026 at 10:30 a.m. at Druces LLP, 6 Gresham Street, London. The company operates three key projects: Wildstone joint-venture in Morocco (50% stake in copper, silver and antimony exploration); Silverking copper-silver project in Zambia; and Bushranger copper-gold project in Australia (100% interest with 599Mt resources). Beaumont Cornish Limited serves as Nominated Adviser.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure of AGM details for a mineral exploration company. No material business announcements or corporate actions disclosed; standard governance communication.

headline: Tirzzit Mapping Identifies New Mineralisation
brief: Talisman Metals discovers new copper mineralisation zone extending 2km east at Tirzzit Project in Morocco.
summary: Talisman's geology team identified outcropping copper mineralisation at its Tirzzit Copper Project, significantly extending the known mineralisation zone 2km eastward from historic drill hole TT6 (11.40m @ 0.60% Cu). The discovery includes malachite within siltstones, uncovered during scouting operations north of the license boundary. Channel sampling is underway with assay results expected within weeks. The sediment-hosted Cu-Ag project in Morocco comprises 16.5km² across two mining licenses; historic drilling (1972-1976) reported intersections of 3.16% Cu over 2.5m and 0.60% Cu over 11.4m. Planned drilling campaign commences 3Q2026.
retail_take: Early-stage exploration success extending strike length positively signals project potential ahead of drilling campaign, though historical data disclaimers and unverified results limit immediate impact. Market likely views this as encouraging but requiring drilling confirmation.

company: iShares MSCI Europe ex-UK Hdgd DIST
headline: Dividend Declaration
brief: iShares MSCI Europe ex-UK Hedged ETF declares interim distribution of 0.1127p per share, payable 12 August 2026.
summary: The iShares MSCI Europe ex-UK GBP Hedged UCITS ETF (Dist) has declared an interim distribution of 0.1127 GBP per share for the current financial year. Ex-dividend date is 3 August 2026, with record date 4 August 2026 and payment date 12 August 2026. The fund provides GBP currency hedging exposure to European equities excluding the UK.
retail_take: Routine dividend distribution from a passive ETF tracking European equities; investors receiving regular income should note ex-date; no material market implications expected.

headline: Grant and Cancellation of Options
brief: Rosslyn Data Technologies replaces employee share options post-fundraise to realign incentives with current share price.
summary: Rosslyn cancelled 8.23m historical share options and granted 13.03m new options on 22 July 2026, following a recent capital raise. CEO Paul Watts received 6.48m new options (1.85p exercise price) replacing 4.90m cancelled options; CFO Ed Riddell received 3.63m new options (1.85p) replacing 2.45m cancelled options. New options vest after three years, expire after ten years, and now represent 11.0% of issued share capital. The Remuneration Committee determined previous awards were no longer reasonable incentives given the changed share price and capital structure.
retail_take: Routine corporate governance action to recalibrate management incentives post-fundraise; no material shareholder dilution concern given replacement nature and three-year vesting, though dilution rises to 11% of share capital.

headline: Blanket Exploration Results
brief: Caledonia discovers previously unknown gold zone at Blanket mine with oxide and sulphide mineralisation suitable for heap leaching.
summary: Surface drilling at Blanket's K-Pits prospect has identified significant near-surface gold mineralisation in a separate mineralized system approximately 200 metres east of existing underground orebodies. Results include 23m at 2.61g/t oxide and 16m at 6.04g/t sulphide. Metallurgical test work shows encouraging preliminary results for heap leaching of oxide material. Caledonia plans to publish an updated resource statement in August 2026 and commence a trial 10,000-tonne heap leach operation in H2 2026 if metallurgical work confirms acceptable recovery.
retail_take: Discovery of a new, potentially lower-cost oxide processing opportunity at an existing mine with encouraging preliminary test results likely reads positively for near-term value creation and operational optionality.

headline: New Reserves Based Lending Facilities Completion
brief: Serica completes $750m reserves-based lending facility, replacing previous $525m facility with improved terms.
summary: Serica Energy completed a new six-year $750m reserves-based lending facility comprising a $500m revolving loan and $250m letter of credit, replacing its previous $525m facility. The oversubscribed facility features 11 international banks at SOFR plus 3.50% interest, with no amortisation until June 2029. The company holds $326m cash and $784m pro forma liquidity, plus a $750m accordion feature for future expansion. An earlier $300m Nordic bond placement was used to repay debt.
retail_take: Strong balance sheet news supporting growth ambitions with extended maturity and improved pricing; demonstrates lender confidence and provides flexibility for organic projects and M&A, likely viewed positively by markets.

headline: 16 Dufour's Place, W1 now 71% let after refresh
brief: GPE's 16 Dufour's Place W1 now 71% let following refresh; four new leases signed generating £2.38m annual rent.
summary: Great Portland Estates has completed four new leases at its prime West End property 16 Dufour's Place, W1, bringing occupancy to 71% with two office floors remaining available. The 9,312 sq ft of new lettings will generate £2.38 million in annual rent at £256 per sq ft, exceeding March 2026 ERV by 1.1%. GPE relocated two customers to 141 Wardour Street and upgraded ground floor amenities including a 20-person boardroom, breakout areas and improved outdoor space.
retail_take: Demonstrates strong leasing momentum in GPE's core West End portfolio and pricing resilience; signals healthy demand for high-quality managed workspace supporting portfolio value and rental growth.

headline: Replacement - Result of Annual General Meeting
brief: HICL corrects typographical error in AGM voting results announcement; all resolutions passed.
summary: HICL Infrastructure PLC issued a replacement RNS on 23 July 2026 correcting a typographical error in total votes cast from its 22 July AGM. All 17 resolutions passed, including 14 ordinary and 3 special resolutions. Notably, resolutions 2 and 5 each received over 20% votes against (23.06% and 20.31% respectively), triggering mandatory shareholder engagement per AIC Code of Corporate Governance. The board will consult shareholders on reasons for dissent.
retail_take: Routine correction with minor governance implication; over-20% dissent on two resolutions suggests some investor concern but poses no material risk as all resolutions passed clearly.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Grafton Group purchased 60,000 shares at £9.53 average price on 22 July as part of £25m buyback programme.
summary: Grafton Group plc repurchased 60,000 ordinary shares on 22 July 2026 through Goodbody Stockbrokers at volume-weighted average price of £9.5336, with a range of £9.4230 to £9.6220. This brings total shares purchased under the £25m buyback programme (announced 30 June) to 1,019,172 shares for cancellation. Trades executed across London Stock Exchange from 08:12 to 16:26 BST in 161 individual transactions.
retail_take: Share buybacks typically support earnings per share and signal management confidence in valuation, though execution at £9.53 reflects recent trading levels. Routine capital allocation activity with no material news implications.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Plus500 repurchased 8,681 shares at 4,056-4,112p as part of ongoing buyback programme.
summary: Plus500 purchased 8,681 ordinary shares on 22 July 2026 at prices ranging from 4,056p to 4,112p, with a volume-weighted average price of 4,088.71p, via Panmure Liberum Limited. The shares will be held in treasury. Following the purchase, 68,942,831 shares remain in issue (excluding treasury), with 45,945,546 shares held in treasury. Total voting rights stand at 68,942,831. This continues the buyback programme announced 9 February 2026.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution under existing programme; modest daily purchase suggesting measured capital return approach. No market-moving implications.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Wetherspoon purchases 250,000 own shares at 684.17p average for cancellation.
summary: JD Wetherspoon plc repurchased 250,000 ordinary shares on 22 July 2026 through Investec Bank plc at a weighted average price of 684.1712 pence per share, with prices ranging from 684.02p to 684.56p. The shares were acquired via two trades on the London Stock Exchange and will be cancelled. The total investment was approximately £1.71 million.
retail_take: Share buybacks typically signal management confidence in valuation and support EPS through reduced share count; routine capital allocation activity with modest positive undertone for shareholders.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Gamma Communications repurchased 15,887 shares on 22 July 2026 at 950-971p as part of its ongoing buyback programme.
summary: Gamma Communications PLC purchased 15,887 ordinary shares on 22 July 2026 through Investec at prices ranging from 950p to 971p, with a weighted average price of 962.47p. This brings total repurchases under the January 2026 buyback programme to 2,778,705 shares. The company intends to cancel these shares. Following settlement, Gamma has 90,985,646 shares in issue and 89,481,972 voting shares (excluding 1,503,674 treasury shares). The announcement also notes the company is in an offer period as of 7 April 2026.
retail_take: Routine buyback execution during offer period. Share cancellations reduce share count and boost earnings per share mechanically. Non-event absent major news context, though offer period restrictions apply.

headline: H1 2026 Trading Update
brief: hVIVO's orderbook more than doubles to £65m on strong commercial momentum, though H1 revenue declined to £16.3m.
summary: hVIVO reported H1 2026 revenue of £16.3m (down from £24.2m in H1 2025) with full-year revenue expected heavily weighted to H2. The orderbook more than doubled to £65m from £30m at year-start, driven by a 45% increase in proposal volumes. H1 adjusted EBITDA expected negative mid-single digit versus £3.0m positive in H1 2025, but H2 2026 EBITDA expected positive. Cash position stood at £13m (31 Dec 2025: £14.3m). Full-year 2026 revenue guidance remains broadly in line with high single-digit growth expectations.
retail_take: Mixed signals: strong orderbook visibility and proposal growth are encouraging, but H1 revenue miss and negative EBITDA show execution lags expectations. Market likely views this as optionality-dependent on H2 delivery; timing risk remains material.

headline: Licence and Option Agreement for Ageotype
brief: capAI licences Ageotype longevity platform from R42 Group with £2m acquisition option over 12 months.
summary: capAI PLC has executed a licence and option agreement with R42 Group LLC for Ageotype, an AI-powered health data platform integrating MRI, blood metabolomics, cancer detection testing and microbiome analysis. This marks capAI's first capMedical vertical platform. capAI receives exclusive worldwide licence for 12 months (expiring July 2027) with discretionary £2m option to acquire underlying IP. R42 retains 20% of net proceeds post-acquisition. Related party transaction approved by independent directors; future IP acquisition would require shareholder approval as substantial property transaction.
retail_take: Entry into healthcare AI represents strategic diversification into high-growth longevity sector (projected 44% CAGR to 2034), but execution risk remains given platform still in beta and R42 relationship creates governance complexity.

headline: Q1 Results
brief: Airtel Africa posts strong Q1 results with 21.1% constant currency revenue growth and 50.1% EBITDA margin, backed by 189M customers and accelerated capex.
summary: Q1 2026 revenue reached $1,853m (31.0% reported, 21.1% constant currency growth). EBITDA grew 24.4% in constant currency to $928m with margin expansion of 206bps to 50.1%. Profit after tax improved to $198m from $156m, though impacted by $37m exceptional finance cost from commercial dispute settlement. Customer base grew 11.6% to 189m, data customers up 15.5% to 87.3m. Airtel Money TPV surged 51.5% to $245bn with 56.5m customers. Capex accelerated to $389m (vs $121m prior year) with 920+ site additions. Leverage improved to 1.7x from 2.2x. Board approved 1% share buyback; $46.6m deployed by quarter-end. Airtel Money IPO planned for London in 2026.
retail_take: Strongly positive: beat on growth across all segments, margin expansion despite energy headwinds, fortress balance sheet (leverage halved), and fintech spinoff signals confidence. Nigeria tariff hikes flowing through, data momentum accelerating, disciplined capex deployment ahead of demand. Market likely reads as premium African telecom story with fintech optionality.

headline: FY2026 Trading Update
brief: SRT Marine Systems reports 49% revenue growth to £116m and 105% profit jump in FY2026, though one project margin pressured by supply chain costs.
summary: SRT Marine Systems delivered FY2026 revenues of £116m (up 49% from £78m) with profit before tax of £10m (up 105% from £4.9m). Gross cash increased to £57m from £9.9m, though £27m is restricted for project performance bonds. The maritime surveillance specialist noted one ongoing project has lower-than-expected margins due to Middle East supply chain disruptions and scope expansion decisions, expected to be offset by future opportunities. The company maintains a £1.8bn pipeline and expresses confidence in achieving market expectations through existing contract execution and new project commencement.
retail_take: Strong top-line growth and margin expansion offset by one underperforming project; large pipeline and positive forward guidance likely support positive sentiment, though margin pressure and supply chain risks warrant caution.

headline: Final Results for the year ended 31 March 2026
brief: Software Circle reports 22% revenue growth to £22.3m and 61% EBITDA growth; acquires two financial services businesses.
summary: Year ended 31 March 2026: Revenue grew 22% to £22.3m (2025: £18.3m), recurring revenue jumped 33% to £16.8m (76% of total). Operating EBITDA surged 61% to £7.6m; Adjusted EBITDA up 83% to £5.8m at 26% margin. Acquired Artificial Intelligence Finance and Broker Information Services, deploying £12.1m capital. Operating cash flow per share doubled to 1.1p. Operating Return on Capital Deployed 25%, Return on Capital Deployed 19%. Portfolio achieved 7% organic revenue growth and 23% organic EBITDA growth. Post-year, secured £25m debt facility plus £10m accordion option.
retail_take: Strong organic growth acceleration across acquired portfolio (23% EBITDA growth), doubled operating cash flow per share, and improved ROCD suggest compounding strategy gaining traction; acquisition-driven inorganic growth maintained with new debt facility provides growth optionality, though net debt jumped to £11.4m.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: B.P. Marsh repurchases 5,000 shares at 678p per share under existing buyback programme.
summary: B.P. Marsh & Partners purchased 5,000 ordinary shares on 22 July 2026 at 678.00p per share, to be held in treasury. Following the transaction, total voting rights number 35,985,512 shares (from issued capital of 37.1m shares, with 1.114m held in treasury). The purchase forms part of the company's previously announced share buyback programme initiated 15 July 2026.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution; modest capital allocation signal but typical for AIM-listed investment company managing shareholder returns; unlikely to move market materially.

headline: RESULTS FOR THE HALF YEAR (HY) ENDED 30 JUNE 2026
brief: Morgan Sindall reports record H1 2026 results with 21% profit growth and raises medium-term targets for Fit Out and Construction divisions.
summary: Morgan Sindall Group delivered H1 2026 adjusted profit before tax of £116.1m, up 21% from £95.9m, on revenue of £2,562m (+8%). Adjusted operating margin expanded 50 basis points to 4.4%. Net cash stood at £418m; interim dividend increased 10% to 55.0p per share. Fit Out and Construction divisions drove performance. Company raised medium-term targets: Fit Out now £100m-£130m annual operating profit (previously £80m-£100m) and Construction operating margin to 3.5%-4.0% (previously 3.0%-3.5%). Partnership Housing faced subdued housing market conditions; full-year profits expected slightly below prior year. Group maintains confidence in 2026 guidance.
retail_take: Strong operational delivery with elevated guidance signals management confidence despite housing headwinds, supported by robust order book (£12.2bn) and improved cash generation. Market likely positive on margin expansion and dividend growth.

headline: Resources at Mbe increased to 1.66m oz gold
brief: Oriole Resources increases Mbe gold resource to 1.66m oz following 50% uplift at MB01-S deposit.
summary: Oriole Resources (AIM: ORR) has updated its JORC Mineral Resource Estimate for the MB01-S deposit at its 50%-owned Mbe project in Cameroon. The updated Inferred MRE stands at 40.1Mt grading 1.01g/t for 1.3m oz contained gold—a ~50% increase from the October 2025 maiden estimate of 870,000oz. Combined with the neighbouring MB01-N deposit (360,000oz at 1.05g/t), total Mbe resources now total 1.66m oz. Both deposits remain open laterally and at depth. The resource, based on 9,305m of drilling and 4,453m of trenching, is constrained within a US$3,200/oz pit shell. Oriole's attributable MRE across all projects now exceeds 1.1m oz.
retail_take: Material upgrade to flagship asset supports narrative of multi-million-ounce potential; resource still Inferred and open, leaving both upside and execution risk. Positive for exploration-stage sentiment but no near-term revenue catalyst.

headline: Full year trading update
brief: CVS Group delivers FY26 revenue growth to £712.8m with adjusted EBITDA of £141.5m, in line with consensus. Australia expansion accelerates.
summary: CVS Group reported FY26 revenue of £712.8m (up 5.9%), with adjusted EBITDA of £141.5m matching market consensus. Like-for-like sales grew 2.1% despite challenging UK conditions. Australia revenue jumped £27.0m to £79.1m, now representing 11% of group revenue and 16% of EBITDA. The company completed six acquisitions in Australia comprising 14 sites for A$91.0m and exchanged contracts on two further deals. Net bank borrowings increased to £199.6m with leverage at 1.63x. A £50m share buyback programme is underway, with £11.7m completed in FY26. CEO Richard Fairman announced retirement intentions on 30 March 2026, with successor search ongoing.
retail_take: Results meet expectations with no earnings surprise, but Australian growth momentum and CMA regulatory clarity provide positive catalysts. Increased leverage for buybacks and potential M&A signals management confidence, though UK market headwinds persist.

headline: Half-year Financial Report
brief: Jupiter reports 36% AUM growth to £73.7bn, driven by positive flows and CCLA acquisition. Underlying PBT up 67% to £50.7m.
summary: Jupiter Fund Management delivered H1 2026 results showing strong momentum: AUM increased 36% to £73.7bn (from £54.0bn at end-2025), net new business of £0.7bn generated, and underlying profit before tax surged 67% to £50.7m (H1 2025: £30.4m). The CCLA acquisition (completed 2 February 2026, £15.0bn AUM added) integrated well with synergy targets upgraded to at least £17m from initial £16m target. Cost:income ratio improved five percentage points to 77%, tracking toward 70% medium-term target. Investment performance strong: 77% of pre-existing Jupiter mutual fund AUM outperformed peer medians over three years. Interim ordinary dividend of 3.7p per share declared, representing 50% of underlying earnings.
retail_take: Strong execution on strategy and acquisition integration, combined with improved profitability and cost discipline, likely to be read positively by markets. Key driver is momentum reversal and scale benefits offsetting lower fee margins from CCLA.

company: City of London Investment Group PLC
headline: Pre-Close Trading Update
brief: CLIG reports 13.9% FuM growth to $12.3bn despite $1.3bn net outflows, driven by market gains and performance.
summary: City of London Investment Group's FuM grew to $12.3 billion (13.9% YoY) despite net outflows of $1.3 billion, primarily from client rebalancing and pension de-risking. Market and investment performance contributed $2.8 billion. CLIM generated $342 million inflows and KIM $224 million, though outflows concentrated in Emerging Markets and Growth Balanced strategies. Second-half outflows improved to $483 million versus $853 million in H1. Final dividend expected in line with prior year; full results due 15 September 2026.
retail_take: Mixed signal: strong asset growth offsets concerning net outflows trend, though improving H2 momentum and robust market performance provide some reassurance on strategy viability and client confidence.

headline: Trading Update
brief: Eleco reports record ARR of £35.5m (up 16%) and strong organic growth of 15% in H1 2026.
summary: Eleco PLC's H1 2026 trading update shows ARR increased 16% to £35.5m with organic growth of 23% to £34.7m. Total recurring revenue rose 14% to £16.9m, representing 85% of total revenue. Headline revenue grew 8% to £19.9m with organic growth of 15%. The company maintained a strong balance sheet with £15.4m cash, no debt, and improved profitability through cost management and the disposal of non-core Veeuze business. Full-year results expected in September.
retail_take: Strong organic growth, record ARR, and solid cash generation with no debt suggest execution on strategy, though headline revenue growth moderated to 8% and guidance remains conservative—likely viewed positively by growth-focused investors.

headline: Distribution Agreement with Boeing Distribution
brief: LPA Group secures Boeing Distribution agreement for general aviation products with £0.5m initial order expected H2 2026.
summary: LPA Group has signed a distribution agreement with Boeing Distribution to sell Red Box Aviation's general aviation product portfolio globally. Boeing Distribution will leverage its established network to scale LPA's aviation business following the 2024 Red Box Aviation acquisition. An initial stocking order of approximately £0.5 million is expected in the second half of 2026. The agreement supports LPA's diversification strategy away from its traditional rail market focus and validates market demand for its products.
retail_take: Strategic win for LPA's aviation pivot with tangible near-term revenue (£0.5m order) and access to Boeing's distribution infrastructure, likely viewed positively as validates Red Box acquisition and growth strategy.

headline: Audited Results for the Year Ended 31 March 2026
brief: Eco Atlantic reports FY2026 results with transformational farm-down deals with BP and Navitas de-risking Atlantic Margin portfolio.
summary: Eco Atlantic ended FY2026 debt-free with US$10.7m cash and US$17.8m equity. Major achievements include: BP farm-down in Namibia (US$2.7m upfront, US$63m max carry), Navitas strategic partnership across Guyana and South Africa (US$2m paid, US$2.5m option fee), and JHI acquisition in Falkland Islands. Net loss of US$7.5m reflects operating costs and warrant liability changes. Company raised US$10m in January 2026 and migrated to LSE SETS platform.
retail_take: Positive signal: de-risking portfolio with world-class operators (BP, Navitas) while retaining meaningful upside; strong balance sheet with no debt supports exploration. Multiple near-term catalysts expected (regulatory approvals, deal closures, drilling) through 2026.

headline: Half-year Financial Report
brief: Centrica reports H1 2026 results with £737m EBITDA, 9% dividend increase to 2.0p, offset by strategic portfolio shifts.
summary: Centrica delivered H1 2026 adjusted EBITDA of £737m (down from £900m), with adjusted EPS of 6.8p. The interim dividend increased 9% to 2.0p, payable 22 September 2026. Decline driven primarily by Spirit Energy asset disposals and production outages. Company progressed infrastructure investments including 850MW Severn CCGT acquisition (£367m), 2.3m smart meters under management, and Sizewell B life extension to 2055 with £70.50/MWh CfD. Transformation programme ramping with £92m H1 investment targeting £500m cost reduction by 2030. Long-term targets unchanged: £2.0bn adjusted EBITDA and doubled EPS by 2030.
retail_take: Mixed signals: dividend resilience and strategic progress offset by EBITDA decline and elevated bad debts (4% of revenue). Market likely views infrastructure pivot positively but remains cautious on near-term retail headwinds and cash generation.

headline: Anglo American Production Report Q2 2026
brief: Anglo American reports flat Q2 copper output at 173kt; revises copper unit costs sharply lower to 145c/lb.
summary: Q2 2026 copper production held steady at 173,200 tonnes versus 173,300 tonnes year-on-year, tracking to plan. Copper Chile output rose 2% to 98,200t driven by Los Bronces 25% production jump following second plant restart, offset by Collahuasi 11% decline due to lower ore grades. Peru's Quellaveco fell 2% to 75,000t reflecting anticipated grade decline. Full-year copper unit cost guidance revised lower to 145c/lb (previously 172c/lb); Chile guidance lowered to 210c/lb (from 230c/lb) and Peru to 65c/lb (from 100c/lb), benefiting from strong by-product credits and favourable FX. Premium iron ore declined 3% to 15.4Mt. H1 2026 copper realised price 608c/lb versus 593c/lb LME average. Full-year production guidance maintained at 700-760kt.
retail_take: Significant cost guidance cuts signal operational efficiency gains and favourable commodity pricing mix, offsetting production flatness. Market likely views cost-out positively despite no volume growth; Teck merger completion (Sept 2026-March 2027) awaiting final China anti-trust approval.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: TRIG buys back 265,000 shares at 74.9p as part of ongoing programme; now holds 154.2m treasury shares.
summary: The Renewables Infrastructure Group purchased 265,000 ordinary shares on 22 July 2026 at a weighted average price of 74.8986p through Investec Bank plc, within its share buyback programme announced August 2024. The shares will be held as treasury stock. TRIG now holds 154,237,374 treasury shares, with 2,331,725,512 voting rights outstanding excluding treasury holdings.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution supporting capital management and earnings per share accretion; demonstrates ongoing shareholder return commitment within established programme parameters.

headline: Trading Update
brief: MAB reports resilient H1 2026 with 16% mortgage completion growth and 8% revenue increase to £160m, though profit flat year-on-year.
summary: Mortgage Advice Bureau delivered H1 2026 mortgage completions of £16.5bn (up 16% from £14.2bn) and revenue of £160m (up 8% from £148.2m), with adjusted profit before tax of £14.6m (H1 2025: £14.5m). Market share in new mortgage lending increased to 8.3% (2025: 8.2%) and Product Transfers to 3.2% (2025: 2.9%). The adviser base grew 3% to 2,194. Profit margins compressed due to product mix shift toward lower-margin remortgages, slower protection volumes, and acquisition integration timing. Management expects full-year results in line with guidance, with H2 profitability weighted higher than H1, supported by 70,000 fixed-rate mortgage maturities and synergy delivery.
retail_take: Volume growth and market share gains are positive, but flat profit despite rising revenue signals margin pressure and execution risk; guidance maintenance lacks conviction given macro headwinds and uncertain housing market recovery outlook.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Pantheon International repurchased 250,000 shares on 22 July 2026 at 387.75-390.00p, intending to cancel them.
summary: Pantheon International PLC purchased 250,000 ordinary shares (6.7p par value) on 22 July 2026 via Investec Bank at a weighted average price of 389.15p, within a range of 387.75p-390.00p. The shares will be cancelled, reducing total issued shares from 393,963,764 to 393,713,764. The transaction represents a routine capital management exercise under the company's buyback programme.
retail_take: Share buyback with cancellation typically signals management confidence in valuation and returns surplus capital to remaining shareholders through increased EPS accretion; market likely views neutrally as modest transaction reflecting capital allocation discipline.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Greencoat Renewables repurchased 250,014 shares at €0.7570-€0.7670 for cancellation on 22 July 2026.
summary: Greencoat Renewables PLC purchased 250,014 ordinary shares on Euronext Dublin on 22 July 2026 as part of its share buyback programme announced 5 March 2026. Shares were bought at prices ranging from €0.7570 to €0.7670, with a volume-weighted average price of €0.7625. Following settlement, the company holds 200,000 treasury shares and has 1,082,584,271 shares in issue. All repurchased shares will be cancelled.
retail_take: Routine capital management execution within announced buyback programme; share cancellation reduces share count, potentially accretive to EPS, but minor single-day tranche suggests ongoing programme implementation rather than material signal.

headline: Update on Offer for Accent
brief: Frasers Group extends takeover offer for Accent Group to 30 September 2026 at A$0.65 per share.
summary: Frasers Group's all-cash takeover offer for Australian-listed Accent Group Limited, announced 15 June 2026 at A$0.65 per share, has been extended to 30 September 2026. The extension was announced via the ASX on 23 July 2026 by Frasers' broker Barrenjoey Markets. No other terms of the offer have changed. The extension provides additional time for acceptance of the bid.
retail_take: Extension suggests slower-than-expected acceptances or regulatory delays, neither validating the offer price nor signalling problems; likely a procedural non-event for most investors.

headline: Update on Arbitration Proceedings against Ukraine
brief: Enwell Energy secures interim protective order in Ukraine arbitration case under ICSID treaty framework.
summary: Enwell Energy (AIM: ENW) has obtained Procedural Order No. 3 from the ICSID arbitration tribunal (dated 26 June 2026) against Ukraine, following disputes over asset freezes, licence suspensions, and operational interference since December 2022. The agreed interim measures require both parties to notify the tribunal within four days of new proceedings affecting Enwell's rights, with reminder of good-faith obligations. The tribunal was constituted in March 2026; Enwell filed its provisional measures request in October 2025.
retail_take: Procedural progress in investor protection dispute; interim order provides some safeguard against further adverse action but represents nascent arbitration stage. Outcome remains highly uncertain and distant; unlikely material positive or negative catalyst near-term.

headline: Issue of Equity in lieu of past director’s fees
brief: Shuka Minerals issues 375,000 shares at 4p each to settle former director's accrued fees.
summary: Shuka Minerals PLC will issue 375,000 ordinary shares priced at 4 pence per share to a former director in settlement of accrued fees owed. Admission to AIM expected 29 July 2026, expanding total issued capital to 143,762,497 shares. The equity issuance avoids cash outlay for historical fee obligations while diluting existing shareholders by approximately 0.26%.
retail_take: Minor dilutive event settling past liabilities through equity rather than cash—routine corporate housekeeping unlikely to move the market materially either way.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Mercia Asset Management repurchased 135,000 shares at 26.50p per share during 16-22 July 2026 under buyback programme.
summary: Mercia Asset Management PLC purchased 135,000 ordinary shares between 16-22 July 2026 at a consistent price of 26.50p, executing its buyback programme announced 1 April 2026. The shares will be cancelled. Following these purchases, issued shares total 421,744,557 (excluding 11.6m treasury shares). The asset manager manages £2.2bn in assets under management and operates 11 regional offices focused on UK SME capital provision.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution at stable pricing; suggests management confidence in valuation but represents modest capital allocation with no material near-term implications for shareholders.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: HarbourVest Global Private Equity cancels 7,000 shares at £33.35 average price following AGM authority.
summary: HarbourVest Global Private Equity Limited (HVPE) purchased 7,000 ordinary shares on 22 July 2026 at an average price of £33.3491 per share for cancellation, exercising authority granted at the 15 July 2026 AGM. Following the transaction, the company has 69,597,352 shares in issue. The denominator figure is provided for shareholders calculating FCA Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules notification thresholds.
retail_take: Routine share buyback and cancellation under prior shareholder approval; modest scale suggests capital management rather than undervaluation signal; non-event for market pricing.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Caledonia Investments buys back and cancels 101,299 own shares at 379p each.
summary: Caledonia Investments purchased 101,299 ordinary shares on 22 July 2026 at 379 pence per share through Peel Hunt LLP, with the shares to be cancelled. The total transaction value was approximately £383.9m. Following the buyback, issued share capital stands at 511,988,074 ordinary shares with voting rights.
retail_take: Share buybacks typically signal management confidence in valuation and can support earnings per share; routine capital management activity likely viewed neutrally by markets.

headline: Half Year Trading Update and Notice of Results
brief: Judges Scientific H1 2026 revenues down 21% YoY to £70.2m due to macro headwinds; FY2026 guidance maintained.
summary: H1 2026 revenue fell 21% to £55.5m, impacted by absent Geotek coring expedition, US federal research funding uncertainties, offshore wind delays, and China R&D tax process delays. Eight businesses grew organically. Order intake down 11% but order book stable at 17.3 weeks. Adjusted EPS expected 38p-42p H1 (vs consensus 202.8p FY2026). Management expects FY2026 in line with market expectations, with benefit from Patent Box relief and lower cost base. Geotek coring expedition now expected 2028 (not early 2027).
retail_take: Negative near-term read: significant H1 revenue miss and delayed major revenue driver (Geotek expedition) push out to 2028, though FY guidance maintained and cost controls in place. Market likely cautious on execution and macro recovery timing.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Edinburgh Investment Trust purchased 85,000 own shares at 828p each on 22 July, to be held in treasury.
summary: Edinburgh Investment Trust PLC bought 85,000 ordinary shares (25p each) on the LSE via Investec at an average price of 828p per share on 22 July 2026. The shares will be held in treasury. Following the purchase, the company holds 69,934,709 treasury shares out of 195,666,734 issued shares, leaving 125,732,025 shares with voting rights.
retail_take: Routine share buyback for treasury holding; common practice for investment trusts managing share count and potential discount to NAV. No material market implications.

headline: Purchase of Own Shares
brief: AdvancedAdvT completes further 5,000 share buyback at 164.91p per share under approved programme.
summary: AdvancedAdvT Limited purchased 5,000 ordinary shares on 22 July 2026 at 164.91 pence per share, bringing cumulative buyback total to 5,284,700 shares since the programme's March 2026 announcement. Shares will be held in treasury. Following the purchase, total voting rights comprise 131,841,106 shares out of 137,125,806 issued shares.
retail_take: Routine buyback execution under previously announced programme; indicates share repurchase continuation but typically signals no major strategic developments or earnings catalyst.

headline: Half Year Report
brief: Howden Joinery H1 2026: Sales +3.3%, underlying operating profit +5.5%, interim dividend +2% to 5.1p. DIY Kitchens acquisition completed post-period.
summary: Howden Joinery delivered H1 2026 sales of £1,030.6m (+3.3% YoY) with underlying operating profit of £128.1m (+5.5%). Gross margin improved 70bps to 62.8% through balanced pricing, volumes and cost efficiencies. Underlying EPS grew 5.5% to 17.3p. Company completed £390m acquisition of DIY Kitchens on 23 June 2026, an online kitchen business generating £136m revenue with 17% five-year growth. Interim dividend increased 2% to 5.1p. Management confirmed full-year 2026 outlook unchanged and £100m share buyback programme on track.
retail_take: Solid operational performance with margin expansion and disciplined cost management supports confidence in guidance. DIY Kitchens acquisition signals growth diversification into complementary online channel, expected accretive immediately. Strong cash generation (£208.3m operating cash flow) and maintained net cash position post-acquisition support shareholder returns.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: IWG repurchased 153,418 shares on 22 July 2026 as part of ongoing £300m+ buyback programme announced December 2025.
summary: International Workplace Group plc purchased 153,418 ordinary shares on 22 July 2026 at volume-weighted average price of £1.9692, via Jefferies International Limited across multiple venues. Since programme launch on 31 December 2025, the company has repurchased 40.5m shares. Post-purchase, 953.8m shares remain in issue (excluding treasury). Shares will be cancelled or held in treasury per shareholder approval at May 2026 AGM.
retail_take: Routine capital management execution within approved buyback remit; neutral signal absent broader context on buyback rationale, cash position, or strategic intent. Market typically reads share repurchases as modestly positive (EPS accretion, capital flexibility) but immaterial absent strategic or valuation catalysts.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares and Total Voting Rights
Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Experian repurchased 28,495 shares on 22 July at average price of 2,682p per share; shares to be cancelled.
summary: Under its June 2026 share repurchase programme, Experian purchased 28,495 ordinary shares on the London Stock Exchange via BofA Securities Europe SA on 22 July 2026. Shares traded between 2,652p and 2,699p, with a weighted average price of 2,682.26p. The purchased shares will be cancelled. Transactions are published weekly on the company website.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution within announced programme; capital return demonstrates confidence but has minimal market impact as regular-course repurchases are well-telegraphed.

headline: Metir Secures Qatar Project Confirmation Update
brief: Metir secures Qatar water monitoring project confirmation with 10-year commitment and expansion prospects.
summary: Modern Water's Continuous Toxicity Monitoring project in Qatar has received formal confirmation from Kahramaa and regional partner Avanceon. Phase 1 handover is reset to October 2026, releasing ~£205,000 in outstanding payments. Post-handover, the contract transitions to recurring consumables revenue (~£30,000/month from 27 units; >£50,000/month if Phase 2's proposed 17 additional systems proceed). Kahramaa committed to 10-year project duration with plans for local reagent production facility.
retail_take: Positive catalyst converting uncertain project into predictable recurring revenue stream with expansion optionality; resolves prior technical concerns and validates core technology in strategically important Middle East market.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Babcock International repurchased 13,548 shares on 22 July 2026 at average price of 1,107.22p as part of ongoing buyback programme.
summary: Babcock International Group PLC purchased 13,548 ordinary shares on 22 July 2026 via J.P. Morgan Securities plc at prices ranging from 1,074.5p to 1,124.5p, with volume-weighted average price of 1,107.22p. Since 1 July 2026, the company has repurchased 886,370 shares costing £8.97m. The company holds 13.46m treasury shares with 492.14m shares in issue.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution within disclosed programme; demonstrates capital discipline and potential EPS accretion, though market likely views as non-material update to previously-announced strategy.

headline: Issue of Shares
brief: Landore Resources issues 880,921 shares worth £16,737.50 to joint broker Hannam & Partners as fee payment.
summary: Landore Resources Limited (AIM: LND) has issued 880,921 ordinary shares to settle fees owed to joint broker Hannam & Partners totalling £16,737.50. The shares will be admitted to AIM trading on or around 28 July 2026. Following admission, the company's total issued share capital will comprise 372,303,404 ordinary shares with voting rights, with no treasury shares held.
retail_take: Routine equity issuance for advisory fees; modest dilution and non-material transaction; typical for small-cap AIM companies managing broker relationships.

headline: Pre-Close Trading Update
brief: Volution expects adjusted EPS of 38.0p, 4% above consensus, with 15% revenue growth and margin expansion across all regions.
summary: Volution Group forecasts adjusted EPS of approximately 38.0p for FY26 ending 31 July 2026, representing 15% growth versus prior year and outperforming market consensus of 36.4p. Total revenue growth of c.15% driven by organic growth of c.3% constant currency plus the AC Industries acquisition in Australasia. All three regions delivered organic operating margin expansion; Continental Europe achieved strongest organic growth of 5.5-6%, while UK remained resilient despite flat full-year revenue. Leverage expected at 1.6x ex-leases, with operating cash conversion above 90% target.
retail_take: Strong pre-close beat on earnings guidance, margin expansion, and maintained organic growth momentum despite challenging conditions signals operational execution strength; market likely views positively as earnings accretion and balance sheet flexibility support continued M&A.

headline: Result of AGM
brief: Record PLC shareholders approve all 15 AGM resolutions with >90% support, including final dividend of 1.45p per share.
summary: Record PLC's 22 July 2026 AGM saw all 15 resolutions pass decisively, with voting support exceeding 90% across the board. Key approvals included adoption of 2026 accounts, a final dividend of 1.45 pence per share, re-election of directors Jan Witte, David Morrison, Matt Hotson, and Krystyna Nowak, appointment of Nick Adams as new director, and re-appointment of BDO LLP as auditor. Special resolutions authorising share issuance, pre-emption rights disapplication, and share buyback authority also passed with strong margins (96-99% support). Shareholder attendance represented 47.2% of issued share capital.
retail_take: Strong governance outcome with overwhelming shareholder endorsement across routine and discretionary matters; dividend confirmation and expanded director authority provide management flexibility. Market likely views this as routine positive housekeeping with no material concerns signalled.

headline: Third Quarter Trading Update
brief: Mitchells & Butlers reports resilient 2.2% YTD like-for-like sales growth despite Q3 headwinds from extreme heat and Easter timing.
summary: The pub and restaurant operator delivered 2.2% year-to-date like-for-like sales growth through 42 weeks to 18 July 2026, though Q3 saw flat performance due to ~0.8ppt Easter timing headwind and extreme heat damaging food-led brands like Toby Carvery and Miller & Carter. Drink-led pubs performed better, boosted by World Cup trading. The company completed 181 conversions and acquired 10 new sites. Cost inflation expectations remain at £120m; management remains confident in delivering full-year consensus expectations.
retail_take: Confidence reaffirmed despite near-term trading pressure; diversified portfolio resilience and investment programme offset headwinds, likely viewed as steady-hand stewardship in challenging conditions.

headline: Half Year Trading Update
brief: Mission Group posts in-line H1 performance with 10% operating profit growth to £2.4m and strong pipeline entering H2.
summary: Mission Group's H1 2026 revenue reached £32.5m (down from £34.1m H1 2025) with headline operating profit up 10% to £2.4m and headline PBT up 27% to £1.4m. The simplified agency portfolio integration and restructuring programme have driven margin improvement. Net bank debt stands at £11.0m (up from £9.0m year-end due to acquisition settlements and restructuring costs). The group maintains confidence in meeting full-year market expectations of £70.0m revenue and £8.1m headline operating profit.
retail_take: Likely read as positive: operating profit and PBT growth despite revenue decline shows successful cost restructuring, while strong pipeline and management confidence support full-year guidance. However, rising debt and revenue decline warrant monitoring.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: IHG repurchased 1,000 shares at $154.25-$156.10 on 22 July under existing authority; shares to be cancelled.
summary: InterContinental Hotels Group purchased 1,000 ordinary shares on 22 July 2026 via Goldman Sachs International on the London Stock Exchange at prices ranging from $154.25 to $156.10, with a volume-weighted average price of $155.49. The shares will be cancelled. Following this transaction, IHG has 148.6 million shares in issue, excluding 5.4 million treasury shares. The repurchase was conducted under shareholder authority granted at the May 2025 AGM.
retail_take: Minimal capital impact given the small 1,000-share purchase size relative to the 154m share base; routine share buyback continuation under existing authority; no material signal for investors.

headline: First Quarter AUM Update
brief: Record PLC reports Q1 AUM of $122.0bn, up 6% QoQ, with positive net flows across all three product pillars.
summary: Record PLC's assets under management reached $122.0 billion at 30 June 2026, up from $114.6 billion on 31 March 2026. Net flows totalled $2.3 billion across Risk Management ($0.7bn), Absolute Return ($0.6bn), and Private Markets ($1.0bn). Asset movements contributed $5.4 billion positively, partially offset by $0.3 billion FX headwinds. Risk Management grew to $98.7 billion, Absolute Return to $4.4 billion, and Private Markets to $18.9 billion. CEO Jan Witte cited strength in Passive Hedging and new FX Alpha mandates, with undeployed commitments of $1.2 billion.
retail_take: Solid organic and market-driven growth across all divisions signals healthy demand and operational momentum, likely viewed positively as AUM drives fee revenues and validates management's strategic direction.

headline: Final Results
brief: Naked Wines reports FY26 adjusted EBITDA of £7.6m, ahead of guidance, with £33.4m net cash and continued profitability focus.
summary: Naked Wines delivered adjusted EBITDA of £7.6m (up 35% constant currency vs FY25's £6.7m), ahead of £5.5m-£7.5m guidance. Revenue declined 20% to £199.1m as the company deliberately reduced unprofitable customer acquisition. Gross margin expanded to 19.9% from 18.4%. Net cash rose to £33.4m (from £30.1m) despite £6m share buybacks. FY27 guidance: revenue £158m-£175m, EBITDA £7.6m-£9.0m, net cash £34m-£42m. Company has delivered £25m annualised savings against £23m medium-term target.
retail_take: Market likely views positively: company prioritising profitability over growth through disciplined cost-cutting and selective customer acquisition, with strong balance sheet and shareholder returns, though deliberate revenue decline signals challenge ahead.

headline: Management Appointments
brief: Empire Metals appoints General Manager (Project Delivery) and Senior Advisor (Approvals) to accelerate Pitfield titanium project development.
summary: Empire Metals appointed Chris Dodds as General Manager (Project Delivery) and Emma Neale as Senior Advisor (Approvals) to advance the Pitfield Titanium Project toward commercial development. Dodds brings 20+ years' experience across mining and critical minerals sectors with roles at Alcoa, Iluka Resources, and Wood. Neale has 14+ years' environmental approvals experience in Western Australia and internationally. The appointments strengthen the in-house team to progress feasibility studies; Pitfield hosts a 2.2 billion tonne resource grading 5.1% TiO₂.
retail_take: Strategic bolstering of operational capacity signals management confidence in project timeline, likely viewed positively as de-risking execution, though remains development-stage without near-term revenue catalysts.

headline: Q3 TRADING UPDATE
brief: ATG raises FY26 revenue guidance to 5.5-6.5% on strong Q3 Arts & Antiques growth and Chairish integration progress.
summary: Auction Technology Group reported Q3 pro forma constant currency revenue growth of 7.8%, with Arts & Antiques showing good momentum from LiveAuctioneers and Chairish, while Industrial & Commercial continued declining at consistent rates. The group upgraded full-year FY26 guidance from 5.0-6.0% to 5.5-6.5% revenue growth, maintaining adjusted EBITDA margin guidance of 34.5-35.5% with leverage expected to improve to 1.5x by year-end from 2.2x in FY25, reflecting healthy cash generation.
retail_take: Market likely sees this positively: upgraded guidance, successful portfolio integration delivering synergies, and strengthened balance sheet signal operational execution and strategic progress, though lower-margin service growth and I&C decline warrant monitoring.

headline: New Natural Hydrogen Contract Win
brief: Getech wins €1m+ European Commission contract to map natural hydrogen potential across EU.
summary: Getech has been awarded a major European Commission contract worth more than €1 million over FY26-FY27 as part of a consortium led by Trinomics. The 12-month study, commissioned by DG GROW, will map underground natural hydrogen reservoirs across all EU Member States, assess commercial extraction feasibility, and provide regulatory recommendations. Getech will deliver geological components using its proprietary gravity/magnetic datasets and Globe platform, generating revenue through consultancy, data and software licensing fees.
retail_take: Financially material contract validates Getech's natural hydrogen expertise and positions it as a thought leader; opens potential for similar studies globally, supporting long-term strategic positioning in emerging energy transition.

headline: Preliminary Results - 52 weeks ended 3 May 2026
brief: TheWorks reports 47% EBITDA growth to £14.0m on 3.3% LFL sales growth; FY27 guidance maintained at £15.0m.
summary: TheWorks delivered FY26 pre-IFRS 16 Adjusted EBITDA of £14.0m (FY25: £9.5m), a 47% increase, on total revenue of £260m with like-for-like sales growth of 3.3%, significantly outperforming the UK non-food retail market decline of 0.1%. Product margin improved 240bps through supplier negotiations and better stock control; the company achieved its planned £2m cost reduction programme. The company closed its loss-making online business in March 2026 (under 10% of sales) to focus on its 508-store estate. Maintained FY27 Adjusted EBITDA guidance of £15.0m with strong momentum continuing, posting 8.8% LFL growth in the first 11 weeks of FY27.
retail_take: Market likely to view positively: strong profit growth, outperformance versus sector, disciplined strategic pivot to profitable stores, and positive early-year trading momentum suggest sustainable recovery in profitability.

headline: FY2027 Q1 performance update
brief: 3i Group reports strong Q1 FY2027 with NAV up 3% to 3,131p; Action continues impressive growth with 14% sales increase.
summary: 3i Group's NAV per share increased to 3,131 pence at 30 June 2026 from 3,030 pence, delivering 3% total return despite £276m FX headwind. Flagship holding Action generated €8,348m net sales and €1,108m operating EBITDA (both 14% and 13% ahead YoY respectively) in the first six months, with 3.6% like-for-like growth. Action added 121 net new stores to reach 3,423 locations and remains on track for 400+ store additions this year. 3i's portfolio also benefited from a £254m dividend from Action and £18m from infrastructure holding 3iN. The group launched a £750m share buyback in May 2026, with £344m deployed by 21 July.
retail_take: Strong operational performance from Action and stable portfolio momentum likely viewed positively by markets, though FX headwinds and valuation multiples unchanged suggest measured investor sentiment; buyback signals confidence in capital allocation.

headline: RELX 2026 INTERIM RESULTS
brief: RELX reports H1 2026 results with 7% underlying revenue growth and 9% profit growth; reaffirms full-year outlook.
summary: RELX delivered H1 2026 revenue of £4,871m (underlying growth +7%) and adjusted operating profit of £1,727m (underlying growth +9%), with adjusted EPS of 68.6p, constant currency growth +11%.
